title: Add schemas for execplan and review JSON records
goal: Implement #505 by adding explicit schemas or schema-backed validation for planning execplan and review JSON records.
Enforce completed/closed closeout and durable-residue fields through planning checks instead of convention-only parsing.
Update structured-file inventory so planning record JSON files are no longer routed as freeform-prohibited gaps.
non_goals: Do not make archived records ordinary startup input.
Do not require Markdown derived views to be schema-backed.
Do not add host-repo or GitHub-specific shipped behavior.
Do not rewrite the planning package around a new framework unless existing schema tooling cannot carry the slice.

intent.outcome: planning execplan and review JSON records have explicit schemas or schema-backed validators, with closeout/durable-residue requirements enforced for completed records
intent.constraints: repo/tool/language agnostic; prefer existing JSON Schema tooling and planning checks; keep archives deep evidence, not first-contact input
intent.latitude: may add schema files, validator helpers, check integration, template metadata, inventory updates, and focused tests
intent.escalation: stop if acceptance requires a new storage format or a broad migration of all historical archives

execution.next_step: archive this slice, close #505, then continue the remaining #502 child issues

invariants: schema validation should support future cross-language generation
completed records must carry enough closeout evidence for restart and audit without forcing archive reads during startup

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: all machine-readable files are schema-backed, typed-validator-backed, generated/derived, or explicitly diagnostic

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Complete the bounded #505 slice with explicit planning record schema validation.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve package boundaries, avoid host-specific shipped behavior, avoid broad archive migration unless tests prove it necessary, and keep #502 open for remaining child issues.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Schema file location, validator helper names, focused test coverage, inventory wording, and proof breadth.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Existing JSON Schema/check paths cannot express the completed-record rules without adding a separate custom validation layer.

active_milestone.scope: planning execplan/review JSON schemas and inventory status

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed slice and continue #506 unless refreshed external evidence reprioritizes the structured-file-contract lane.
validation_commands: cd packages/planning && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q
cd packages/planning && uv run ruff check .
completion_criteria: Execplan records validate against an explicit schema or schema-backed validator.
Review records validate against an explicit schema or schema-backed validator.
Completed/closed records require closeout and durable-residue fields through validation/checks.
Invalid record tests fail with actionable diagnostics.
Structured-file inventory marks planning record JSON files schema-backed or typed-validator-backed instead of freeform-prohibited gaps.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Planning execplan and review JSON records are schema-backed via planning package payload schemas, and the planning surface checker now validates live and archived records against those schemas.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Planning records are restart and audit surfaces; arbitrary field drift makes recovery and future code generation harder.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: when planning record shape, archive closeout requirements, or review artifact structure changes

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Added JSON Schema definitions for planning execplan and review records, installed them through the planning package payload, enforced them in planning checks/archive validation, and updated structured-file inventory so planning record files are schema-backed.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: pytest and ruff passed for workspace CLI, structured-file inventory, and planning package tests; planning surface and contract inventory checks passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The schemas, checker integration, tests, and inventory entries are durable checked-in owners; the archive is proof/evidence only.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No Memory promotion needed; package README and payload classification were updated because shipped payload changed.
execution_summary.resume from: Refresh external evidence, close #505, then continue #506 unless summary reprioritizes.

signals routed: Payload inventory warnings around optional planning payload extras remain under the existing structured-file/package classification work rather than a new issue.
Subagent code/inventory mapping reduced main-thread discovery; no package change needed beyond continuing to use checked-in handoff and summary surfaces.
signals dismissed: The root and package-bootstrap schema copies are duplicate-looking, but they are required by the existing package payload/install model and covered by maintainer-surface checks.

discard.summary: Detailed command output and subagent exploration notes are not durable operating knowledge beyond the validation list.

continuation.summary: Continue #506/#508/#509 under the #502 structured-file-contract parent lane.

memory.summary: No Memory promotion needed; package README and payload classification were updated because shipped payload changed.

config_check.summary: planning surface checker validates planning record schemas for live and archived records
No Memory promotion needed; package README and payload classification were updated because shipped payload changed.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #505 delivered schema-backed execplan and review record validation, planning checker/archive validation, package payload schema installation, inventory updates, and passing proof.
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: #506/#508/#509 remain open under #502 for remaining structured-file contract work.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: Bounded #505 slice is complete; #502 parent lane remains open for remaining child issues.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; cd packages/planning && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q; cd packages/planning && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py; cd packages/planning && uv run ruff check .; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py --strict; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #505 if planning .plan.json/.review.json records stop validating through package-shipped schemas, arbitrary top-level fields become accepted without schema support, or completed planning records can bypass closeout evidence checks.

proof_report.proof achieved now: yes; validation evidence is recorded in execution summary.
